cultural_note_en: A Cantonese dim-sum classic from the teahouses of Guangzhou, here in an upscale black-truffle version. A translucent wheat-starch skin, pleated by hand into at least seven folds, wraps a filling of whole shrimp and black truffle; each dumpling is finished with a shaving of truffle on top and a brush of truffle oil. Steamed to order and best eaten while the skin is still glossy and springy.
